blocking_findings.summary: Root checker still directly imported WORKBENCH_MOUNT_PREFIX, blurring the runtime/API boundary.
Root checker scenario coverage was implicit and did not visibly prove all 15 scenarios were still mapped and executed.
Projected/mobile evidence risked recording hardcoded projection/page values instead of page-derived evidence.

changes: Root checker now builds mounted API URLs through workbenchApiUrl exported by tools/workbench-browser-events-runtime.mjs.
Root checker now declares SCENARIO_GROUPS with explicit scenario names and asserts required, actual, missing, unexpected, and duplicate scenario coverage after execution.
Projected/mobile/durable scenarios now record page-derived readout, locator, body, and dimension evidence; expected_* fields are labeled as expectations rather than page measurements.
Browser-events run artifact was regenerated after remediation and still records 15 scenarios.

notes: Root imports workbenchApiUrl from runtime and no longer imports WORKBENCH_MOUNT_PREFIX directly.
SCENARIO_GROUPS maps all 15 scenarios and assertScenarioContract validates full coverage and duplicate absence.
Hardcoded evidence blocker is closed: mobile uses body text, locators, readouts, and dimensions; durable expected_* fields are marked as expectations.
Parity records a 227-line root and extracted modules no larger than 254 lines, with 15 required and 15 actual scenarios.
Manifest/parity evidence remains compatible with the below-500 policy.

synthesis_arbitration.note: The final synthesis status was pass with no blocking findings. One synthesis note said projected mobile/durable evidence was unverified from compressed shard summaries, but the second shard directly reviewed tools/workbench-browser-events-projected-scenarios.mjs and explicitly marked that blocker closed; this evidence file treats the direct shard result as authoritative for that item.

supplemental_test_fix_review.reason: `npm test` exposed that test/workbench-shell.test.js still read the pre-extraction root checker for semantic scheduler-loop readout guards.
notes: DeepSeek confirmed the test change follows the new module boundary rather than weakening assertions.
The test still checks the semantic copy constants, raw fail-closed token sets, key raw tokens, fail-closed token-return pattern, and projected-scenario use of the semantic helper functions.
The test still rejects weak direct comparisons such as resumedLoopRecovery === "空闲" and resumedLoopAttempt === "未配置".
DeepSeek confirmed the legacy-static inventory test now strengthens the mount-prefix boundary: root checker must not contain WORKBENCH_MOUNT_PREFIX and runtime must contain it.
DeepSeek confirmed the legacy static fail-closed checks remain intact, including retired static behavior, no legacy static serving, and no desktop.html/mobile.html navigation in current gates.

required_follow_up: Run final repository gates before commit and merge.
